mission.title: Install and Configure ChaosBlade on Kubernetes
mission.description: ChaosBlade is an Alibaba open source chaos engineering tool that follows chaos engineering principles to help enterprises improve the fault tolerance of distributed systems. The chaosblade-operator enables Kubernetes-native chaos experiments defined as CRDs.

steps.title: Download the ChaosBlade CLI
steps.description:
Download the ChaosBlade CLI toolkit from the official releases.
```bash
curl -fsSL https://github.com/chaosblade-io/chaosblade/releases/download/v1.8.0/chaosblade-1.8.0-linux_amd64.tar.gz -o chaosblade.tar.gz
tar -xzf chaosblade.tar.gz
sudo mv chaosblade-1.8.0 /opt/chaosblade
export PATH=$PATH:/opt/chaosblade
```

steps.title: Install the ChaosBlade Operator using Helm
steps.description:
Clone the operator repo and install it via Helm to enable Kubernetes-native chaos experiments.
```bash
kubectl create ns chaosblade
git clone https://github.com/chaosblade-io/chaosblade-operator.git
cd chaosblade-operator/deploy/helm
helm package ./chaosblade-operator
helm install chaosblade chaosblade-operator-*.tgz --namespace chaosblade
```

steps.title: Verify the operator installation
steps.description:
Check that the ChaosBlade operator pods are running in the chaosblade namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace chaosblade
kubectl get crd | grep chaosblade
```

steps.title: Run a sample chaos experiment
steps.description:
Create a sample ChaosBlade experiment to verify everything works.
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: chaosblade.io/v1alpha1
kind: ChaosBlade
metadata:
  name: cpu-load-test
spec:
  experiments:
  - scope: pod
    target: cpu
    action: fullload
    desc: "CPU full load test"
    matchers:
    - name: names
      value:
      - "your-pod-name"
    - name: namespace
      value:
      - "default"
    - name: cpu-percent
      value:
      - "50"
EOF
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the ChaosBlade operator running in the chaosblade namespace with the ChaosBlade CRDs registered. You can then define chaos experiments as Kubernetes resources.

uninstall.title: Remove all chaos experiments
uninstall.description: Delete all ChaosBlade experiment resources before uninstalling the operator.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Helm release
uninstall.description: Remove the chaosblade-operator Helm release from the chaosblade namespace.

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and namespace
uninstall.description: Delete the ChaosBlade CRDs and the chaosblade namespace.

upgrade.title: Download the latest CLI
upgrade.description: Download the latest ChaosBlade CLI release from GitHub.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the operator
upgrade.description: Rebuild the Helm chart from the latest operator source and run helm upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Operator pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
Check the operator logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=chaosblade-operator --namespace chaosblade
```

troubleshooting.title: Chaos experiment stuck in pending
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the target pod exists and the namespace matches the experiment spec.
```bash
kubectl describe chaosblade cpu-load-test
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, along with Helm, kubectl, and git installed.
